hero.summary: Specialized in React and Node.js, with over 5 years of experience in EdTech. Used to working fully remote within agile teams, I build performant, accessible and scalable applications. Autonomous, curious and passionate about best practices, I love turning complex needs into simple and effective solutions.

projects.title: Realizations
projects.subtitle: Concrete projects, real problems, lasting solutions.

playground.title: Playground
playground.subtitle: Accessible React components, tested and documented. Keyboard navigation, ARIA, focus management — best practices in action.

modal.title: Accessible Modal
modal.desc: Focus trap, scroll lock, focus return, Escape to close.
modal.demoDesc: This modal implements all accessibility best practices:

features.focusReturn: Focus returns to trigger on close

featureList.focusTrap: Focus trap — focus stays in the modal

toast.title: Toast Notifications
toast.desc: Queue system, aria-live, auto and manual close.

accordion.title: Accessible Accordion
accordion.desc: Full keyboard navigation, aria-expanded, single or multi open.

a11y.title: ♿ Why web accessibility?
a11y.content: 15% of the world's population lives with a disability. An accessible interface benefits everyone — screen reader users, keyboard navigation, elderly people, low connectivity contexts. It's a right, not an option.

aria.title: 🏷 ARIA — when and how to use it?
aria.content: ARIA (Accessible Rich Internet Applications) enhances semantic HTML. Golden rule: don't use ARIA if native HTML suffices. A <button> is always preferable to a <div role="button">.
aria.rule: don't use ARIA if native HTML suffices

focus.title: ⌨️ Keyboard focus management
focus.content: Every interactive element must be reachable by keyboard (Tab) and have a visible focus indicator. Modals require a focus trap — focus must not escape to background content.

contrast.title: 🎨 Contrast and colors
contrast.content: WCAG 2.1 level AA requires a contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text. Never convey information through color alone.
